notes: PROFILE 1014: 1L crizotinib vs pemetrexed/platinum in ALK+ non-squamous NSCLC. No prior systemic therapy for advanced disease; prior ALK-targeted therapy excluded.
ARCHER 1050: 1L dacomitinib vs gefitinib in EGFR-mutant (ex19del or L858R) advanced NSCLC; treatment-naive in advanced setting.
Phase I/II of alectinib in ALK+ NSCLC after crizotinib failure; required prior crizotinib with progression.
AURA Phase I/II of AZD9291 (osimertinib) in EGFR-mutant NSCLC progressed on prior EGFR TKI (1st/2nd gen); includes T790M+ and T790M-unknown cohorts. 1L expansion cohort also enrolled.
ASCEND-4: 1L ceritinib (LDK378) vs pemetrexed/platinum in ALK+ non-squamous NSCLC, treatment-naive.
ASCEND-5: ceritinib vs pemetrexed or docetaxel in ALK+ NSCLC post platinum-doublet and crizotinib.
Open-label afatinib for treatment-naive 1L or chemotherapy-pretreated EGFR-mutant NSCLC; mixed-line population so post_egfr_tki marked false as primary state.
Phase II gefitinib + metformin vs gefitinib + placebo in 1L EGFR-mutant advanced NSCLC. Experimental arm is gefitinib + metformin (off-class combination), mapped to investigational (other).

Phase II emibetuzumab (anti-MET LY2875358) + erlotinib vs erlotinib alone as 1L in EGFR-mutant NSCLC after 8-week erlotinib lead-in. Experimental arm is anti-MET + EGFR TKI combination, mapped to investigational (other).
Out of scope: ROS1-rearranged, ALK-negative NSCLC (East Asian); single-arm crizotinib. Deferred to v4.

trial_name: Gefitinib +/- chemo for EGFR-mut NSCLC brain mets
notes: Phase III gefitinib monotherapy vs gefitinib + pemetrexed/platinum in EGFR-mutant NSCLC with brain metastases, treatment-naive. Experimental arm is gefitinib-based (chemo-augmented); primary class is 1st-gen EGFR TKI.
Single-arm afatinib safety study in EGFR-mutant locally advanced/metastatic NSCLC, prior EGFR TKI excluded.
Randomized phase II erlotinib 100mg vs gefitinib 250mg in EGFR-mutant (ex19del or L858R) advanced NSCLC; no prior EGFR-directed therapy.

Lorlatinib (PF-06463922) phase I/II in ALK+ or ROS1+ NSCLC; ALK-positive cohorts include treatment-naive (Phase 2) and post-crizotinib +/- chemotherapy, and post >=1 ALK inhibitor (Phase 1). Also enrolls ROS1+ patients (out-of-scope subgroup), but trial primarily for ALK-resistance landscape; assigned NSCLC-ALK.
Single-arm ceritinib (LDK378) in Chinese ALK+ NSCLC patients with progression on prior crizotinib.
ALEX: 1L alectinib vs crizotinib in treatment-naive ALK+ advanced NSCLC.
AURA2: single-arm AZD9291 (osimertinib) in T790M+ EGFR-mutant NSCLC post prior EGFR TKI.
ALTA: randomized phase II brigatinib in ALK+ NSCLC with progression on crizotinib.

EGF816/nazartinib is investigational 3rd-gen EGFR TKI (T790M-targeting). Phase I (post-EGFR TKI) and Phase II (1L EGFR TKI naive) cohorts in EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Mapped to 3rd-gen class even though not osimertinib (label-matches 3rd-gen).
v3 round-1 corrected: original supplementary NCT02788279 was IMblaze370 (colorectal); real AURA3 is NCT02151981.
TIGER-1: 1L rociletinib (CO-1686, investigational 3rd-gen EGFR TKI) vs erlotinib in EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Exon 20 insertion excluded except A763_Y764insFQEA. Mapped to 3rd-gen class.
AC0010 (avitinib) is investigational 3rd-gen EGFR TKI targeting T790M. Required progression on prior EGFR TKI. Mapped to 3rd-gen class.
FLAURA: 1L osimertinib vs gefitinib/erlotinib in EGFR-mutant (ex19del or L858R) advanced NSCLC, treatment-naive.
TIGER-3: rociletinib (investigational 3rd-gen EGFR TKI) vs single-agent chemotherapy in EGFR-mutant NSCLC after at least 1 prior EGFR TKI and a platinum-doublet. Mapped to 3rd-gen class.
AC0010 (avitinib) in T790M+ EGFR-mutant advanced NSCLC after prior EGFR TKI. Investigational 3rd-gen EGFR TKI; mapped to 3rd-gen class.

Phase Ib/II nazartinib (EGF816) + capmatinib (INC280) combination in EGFR-mutant NSCLC across multiple groups (EGFR TKI-resistant, de novo T790M, TKI-naive). Combination 3rd-gen EGFR TKI + MET inhibitor; not in canonical class list, mapped to investigational (other).
ASCEND-7: ceritinib in ALK+ NSCLC with brain/leptomeningeal metastases (5-arm: includes pretreated and naive subgroups, with or without prior ALK TKI/radiotherapy). Mixed prior-state population.
Phase III gefitinib + thalidomide (+/- aspirin) vs gefitinib + placebo in EGFR-mutant NSCLC, treatment-naive. Experimental arm is off-class gefitinib + thalidomide; mapped to investigational (other).

RELAY: 1L ramucirumab + erlotinib vs placebo + erlotinib in EGFR-mutant (ex19del or L858R) NSCLC; T790M known excluded; later expansion arms with gefitinib/osimertinib also added per intervention list.
Out of scope: GEOMETRY mono-1 enrolls EGFR wild-type, ALK-negative, MET-altered (METex14/amplified) advanced NSCLC; capmatinib monotherapy. MET ex14-skip target is deferred to v4.

S1403: 1L afatinib + cetuximab vs afatinib alone in treatment-naive EGFR-mutant (ex19del or L858R) advanced NSCLC. Experimental arm afatinib + cetuximab; canonical class for afatinib backbone (2nd-gen).
AURA17: single-arm AZD9291 (osimertinib) in Asia-Pacific T790M+ EGFR-mutant NSCLC post prior EGFR TKI.

Out of scope: MYSTIC enrolls driver-negative (EGFR/ALK-negative) NSCLC for 1L durvalumab +/- tremelimumab vs platinum-doublet chemo. Driver-negative immunotherapy trial, deferred to v4. Drug class mapped for completeness.
ASTRIS: real-world single-arm AZD9291 (osimertinib) in T790M+ EGFR-mutant advanced NSCLC post prior EGFR TKI.
Single-arm osimertinib in EGFR-mutant NSCLC after >=1 prior EGFR TKI, including T790M-positive subset; coupled to extensive translational studies.
Out of scope: NEPTUNE enrolls driver-negative (no activating EGFR mut, no ALK rearrangement) NSCLC; 1L durvalumab + tremelimumab vs platinum-doublet. Deferred to v4.

Out of scope: STARTRK-2 is a basket of solid tumors with NTRK1/2/3, ROS1, or ALK rearrangements; entrectinib monotherapy. NTRK/ROS1 fusions are deferred to v4; ALK subgroup is mixed with non-NSCLC tumors. Marked NSCLC-other / low confidence.
ASP8273 (naquotinib) is investigational 3rd-gen EGFR TKI. Phase III 1L vs erlotinib or gefitinib in EGFR-activating-mutation NSCLC. Mapped to 3rd-gen class.
ALUR: alectinib vs pemetrexed or docetaxel in ALK+ NSCLC after prior platinum-based chemotherapy and crizotinib.
BEVERLY: 1L bevacizumab + erlotinib vs erlotinib alone in EGFR-mutant non-squamous NSCLC. T790M-only and exon 20 insertions excluded.

Out of scope: TAK-788 (mobocertinib) targets EGFR exon 20 insertions and HER2 exon 20 insertions. EGFR ex20ins-directed therapy not in canonical NSCLC class list; HER2-mutant NSCLC is OOS per protocol. Tumor type EGFR-altered but ex20ins-specific; assigned NSCLC-other / low confidence.
Randomized phase II pemetrexed alone vs pemetrexed + cisplatin in EGFR-mutant NSCLC after acquired resistance to 1st-line EGFR TKI (gefitinib/erlotinib/icotinib).
ALTA-1L: 1L brigatinib vs crizotinib in treatment-naive ALK+ advanced NSCLC.
PLASMA: single-arm osimertinib (AZD9291) in EGFR-mutant NSCLC with T790M detected in plasma ctDNA after prior EGFR TKI.

Treatment-naive EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) non-squamous NSCLC; gefitinib + apatinib (anti-VEGFR2 TKI) vs gefitinib + placebo. First-line setting; chemotherapy and targeted therapy excluded. Lead arm classified by EGFR TKI 1st-gen backbone (apatinib is anti-angiogenic TKI but no canonical class fits exactly).
Asian phase 3 alectinib vs crizotinib in treatment-naive ALK+ advanced NSCLC. No prior systemic treatment for advanced disease allowed.
EORTC APPLE: feasibility of monitoring plasma T790M with osimertinib treatment strategy in 1L EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) NSCLC. EGFR TKI-naive required.

Phase 3 EGFR-TKI +/- SBRT in newly diagnosed oligometastatic EGFRm NSCLC. Sensitizing EGFR mutations (L858R, Ex19del, G719A, L861Q, S768I, Ex19 ins); TKI-naive (erlotinib lead-in <=12wks allowed). SBRT is local therapy, drug arm is EGFR TKI 1st-gen (erlotinib).

First-line thymosin alpha-1 bio-immunotherapy combined with standard-of-care EGFR-TKI in sensitizing EGFRm NSCLC. Out of scope: investigational immunomodulator not in canonical class list. Closest = investigational (other) with EGFR-TKI backbone.

Bim-deletion or low-abundance EGFR-mutation subgroup (Ex19del/L858R) 1L gefitinib + chemo or anti-angiogenesis. TKI-naive. Backbone EGFR TKI 1st-gen; combination arm could be Bevacizumab+EGFR TKI or chemo, but lead/backbone is gefitinib.
3rd-line osimertinib vs docetaxel+bevacizumab in EGFR T790M+ NSCLC after prior TKI and chemotherapy. No prior anti-VEGF allowed.
AZD9291 (osimertinib) in brain mets/leptomeningeal disease from T790M+ EGFRm NSCLC after prior EGFR-TKI. Prior osimertinib within 6 months excluded.

Almonertinib (HS-10296, 3rd-gen EGFR TKI analog to osimertinib) in patients with prior EGFR-TKI progression and T790M+ status. Sensitizing mutations including G719X, Ex19del, L858R, L861Q. Classified under 3rd-gen EGFR TKI class.
ADELOS: plasma T790M testing methodology study in Chinese NSCLC patients post-EGFR-TKI receiving osimertinib (AZD9291). Sparse eligibility text but clearly post-EGFR-TKI.
Out of scope: PD-L1-high, EGFR/ALK driver-negative 1L NSCLC (durvalumab vs platinum chemo). Driver-negative NSCLC is OOS per protocol but retained for transparency.
Lazertinib (YH25448) phase 1/2 in EGFRm advanced NSCLC; T790M+ confirmed after progression on prior EGFR-TKI. Lazertinib is a 3rd-gen EGFR TKI, classified under osimertinib class.
CROWN: phase 3 lorlatinib vs crizotinib in 1L ALK+ advanced NSCLC. No prior systemic therapy for advanced disease.
Erlotinib +/- bevacizumab in 1L EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) advanced non-squamous NSCLC. Treatment-naive. Experimental arm = Bevacizumab + EGFR TKI.

BOOSTER: 2L osimertinib +/- bevacizumab in EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) T790M+ NSCLC after prior EGFR-TKI (erlotinib/gefitinib/dacomitinib/afatinib). Up to 1 prior platinum chemo allowed. Prior osimertinib excluded. Experimental arm osimertinib + bevacizumab; classified by 3rd-gen TKI backbone since no canonical 'osimertinib + bevacizumab' class exists.
Out of scope: APL-101 (vebreltinib) in c-MET Exon 14 skipping mutations / MET amplification / MET fusion NSCLC and basket tumors. MET-driver NSCLC is OOS for v3 per protocol.
ELIOS: molecular profiling, 1L osimertinib in EGFRm locally advanced/metastatic NSCLC. Excludes Ex20ins, prior EGFR-TKI/cytotoxic for advanced disease, prior immunotherapy.

Phase 2 randomized: gefitinib alone vs gefitinib + chemo vs gefitinib + anti-angiogenesis in Bim-deletion or low-abundance EGFR-mut (Ex19del/L858R) NSCLC. Treatment-naive. Backbone = gefitinib (1st-gen).

AC0010 (avitinib) phase 2 in EGFR T790M+ NSCLC after 1st-gen EGFR-TKI progression (erlotinib/gefitinib/icotinib). Up to 1 chemo allowed. Prior 3rd-gen TKI (osimertinib, etc.) excluded. AC0010 is a 3rd-gen EGFR TKI; classified under osimertinib class.

Out of scope: poziotinib in EGFR or HER2 Exon 20 insertion NSCLC (ZENITH20). EGFR Ex20ins / HER2-mutant are flagged for v4 deferral but retained. Mixed cohorts include 1L and 2L+ and post-osimertinib (Cohort 6).
Gefitinib +/- thalidomide in 1L EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) advanced NSCLC. Treatment-naive. Backbone EGFR TKI 1st-gen (no canonical class for + thalidomide).

BPI-7711 (rezivertinib, 3rd-gen EGFR TKI) phase 1/2a in EGFRm NSCLC. Phase I: post-1st/2nd-gen TKI T790M+; Phase IIa: treatment-naive (sensitizing mutations G719X, Ex19 del, L858R, L861Q). Mixed cohorts; classified by predominant T790M post-TKI arm and 3rd-gen TKI class.

Gefitinib continuation beyond progression in EGFRm NSCLC (Ex18-21 except T790M) who had prior CR/PR >=4mo or SD >=6mo with 1L/2L gefitinib. Patient was already on gefitinib (post-EGFR-TKI), continuing same drug. Excludes T790M+.

J-ALTA: brigatinib in Japanese ALK+ NSCLC. Cohorts: Safety lead-in (any ALK-TKI line), Refractory (post-alectinib +/- crizotinib), and TKI-naive expansion. Main classification = post-ALK-TKI (refractory cohort is primary).

Alflutinib (furmonertinib, 3rd-gen EGFR TKI) phase 2b in T790M+ NSCLC after progression on 1st/2nd-gen EGFR-TKI. Excludes prior 3rd-gen (osimertinib etc.) and Ex20ins. Classified under osimertinib class.
S-1 (oral fluoropyrimidine) + gefitinib vs gefitinib monotherapy in EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) 1L non-squamous NSCLC. Treatment-naive. Backbone EGFR TKI 1st-gen.
Single-arm 1L osimertinib in EGFRm non-squamous NSCLC (any EGFR mutation except Ex20ins). EGFR-TKI naive, no prior systemic for advanced disease.

ASK120067 (limertinib, 3rd-gen EGFR TKI) in EGFR T790M+ NSCLC post-1st/2nd-gen EGFR-TKI (gefitinib/erlotinib/afatinib/icotinib). Sensitizing mutations (G719X, Ex19del, L858R, L861Q). Excludes prior 3rd-gen TKIs.

KEYNOTE-789: pemetrexed + platinum +/- pembrolizumab in TKI-resistant EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) metastatic non-squamous NSCLC. Required EGFR-TKI progression; post-1st/2nd-gen patients must have absence of T790M; post-1st/2nd-gen with T790M+ require prior osimertinib failure; post-1L-osimertinib eligible regardless of T790M. Therefore post_osimertinib=true (at least subset; effectively any TKI-resistant including post-osimertinib).

LAURA: osimertinib maintenance after definitive chemoradiation in unresectable stage III EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) NSCLC. EGFR-TKI naive (no prior EGFR-TKI). Post-chemoradiation setting (chemoradiation does not count as systemic for advanced disease).

ALTA-2: brigatinib in ALK+ NSCLC progressed on alectinib or ceritinib (>=12 weeks on prior ALK TKI). Up to 3 prior systemic regimens allowed; excluded prior ALK-TKI other than alectinib/ceritinib/crizotinib.
Single-arm osimertinib + aspirin in T790M+ EGFRm NSCLC post-1st-gen EGFR-TKI. No prior chemotherapy allowed. Classified under 3rd-gen TKI backbone.

Out of scope: CITYSCAPE — tiragolumab (anti-TIGIT) + atezolizumab in chemo-naive PD-L1+ NSCLC. Excludes EGFR-sensitizing or ALK-fusion driver. Driver-negative NSCLC OOS per protocol. Closest class is PD-1/PD-L1 monotherapy backbone (atezolizumab). Retained for transparency.
ALTA-3: phase 3 brigatinib vs alectinib in ALK+ NSCLC progressed on crizotinib. Only prior ALK-TKI is crizotinib (>=4wks). Up to 2 prior systemic regimens (besides crizotinib) allowed.

AZD3759 (zorifertinib, CNS-penetrant EGFR TKI) vs SoC EGFR-TKI in 1L EGFRm (L858R, Ex19del) NSCLC with brain mets. Treatment-naive. AZD3759 is an investigational 1st/2nd-gen-like EGFR-TKI with CNS penetration; not a canonical 3rd-gen (osimertinib) drug; classified as investigational (other).

Phase 3 EGFR-TKI +/- early SBRT to primary tumor in oligometastatic EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) NSCLC. Treatment-naive (no prior radiotherapy, chemo, immunotherapy or chest surgery). Drug class not specified; assumed 1st-gen (gefitinib/erlotinib) per typical Chinese practice at start year 2019.

SAVANNAH: osimertinib + savolitinib in EGFRm+ / MET+ NSCLC after progression on 1L osimertinib. Only prior 1L osimertinib allowed in metastatic setting. Combo includes MET inhibitor (savolitinib); not osimertinib monotherapy. No canonical class matches osimertinib + MET inhibitor; closest is investigational (other).

FLAG: phase 3 alflutinib (furmonertinib) vs gefitinib as 1L therapy in EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) advanced NSCLC. Treatment-naive. Alflutinib is 3rd-gen EGFR TKI; experimental arm classified under osimertinib class.

ORIENT-31: phase 3 sintilimab +/- IBI305 (anti-VEGF) + pemetrexed/cisplatin in EGFRm non-squamous NSCLC after EGFR-TKI failure. Prior chemo for advanced disease excluded; prior anti-PD-1/L1 excluded. Experimental arm = chemo + PD-1 (+/- anti-VEGF); classified as Platinum doublet + PD-1/PD-L1.
FIOL: single-arm 1L osimertinib in EGFRm (Ex18-21 except Ex20ins) advanced NSCLC with translational studies. No prior systemic for metastatic disease; prior osimertinib excluded.

BPI-7711 (rezivertinib) phase 2b single-arm in T790M+ EGFRm NSCLC after progression on 1st/2nd-gen EGFR-TKI. Sensitizing mutations G719X, Ex19del, L858R, L861Q. Excludes Ex20ins and prior 3rd-gen TKI.

SH-1028 (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) phase 2 in T790M+ EGFRm NSCLC post-EGFR-TKI (Part A) and also treatment-naive cohort (Part B). Primary cohort post-TKI T790M+; mixed cohorts. Excludes prior 3rd-gen TKI (osimertinib, etc.).

AENEAS-like: phase 3 almonertinib (HS-10296, 3rd-gen EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib in 1L EGFRm (Ex19del/L858R) NSCLC. Treatment-naive (no prior systemic for advanced disease, no prior EGFR-TKI). Experimental arm classified under osimertinib class.

Eligibility text mentions 'stage IV NSCLC and ALK fusion' as inclusion (likely a typo / abnormal eligibility — JS001/toripalimab is a PD-1 inhibitor and typical regimen would be 1L driver-negative). Out of scope: ALK+ patients being given chemo + anti-PD-1 1L is non-standard, eligibility ambiguous. If interpreted literally (ALK+), this is 1L ALK+ with chemoimmunotherapy (unusual). Closest class Platinum doublet + PD-1/PD-L1.
Phase 3 first-line trial of abivertinib (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib in patients with sensitizing EGFR mutations (Ex19del or L858R). Prior EGFR TKI excluded.

trial_name: D-0316 in T790M+ EGFR NSCLC after 1st/2nd-gen TKI
notes: Phase 2 of D-0316 (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) in T790M+ NSCLC after progression on 1st/2nd-gen EGFR TKI. Prior 3rd-gen TKI excluded.
MELROSE: phase 2 first-line osimertinib in treatment-naive advanced EGFR-mutant NSCLC (Ex19del/L858R). Studies acquired resistance mechanisms.
Phase 3 of BPI-7711 (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib first-line. Common sensitizing EGFR mut required; T790M-positive primary excluded; exon 20 insertions excluded.
Randomized phase 2 of osimertinib + ramucirumab vs osimertinib alone, TKI-naive EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Previous chemo/IO allowed; prior EGFR TKI and prior anti-VEGF excluded.
Phase 2 dual-cohort lorlatinib monotherapy in ALK+ NSCLC after prior ALK inhibitor (Cohort 1: post-crizotinib only; Cohort 2: post 2G ALK TKI).
Phase 3 pemetrexed + platinum +/- toripalimab in EGFR-mutant NSCLC after EGFR-TKI failure (post-1st/2nd-gen without T790M, or post-osimertinib regardless of T790M status).

INSIGHT 2: tepotinib + osimertinib in MET-amplified, EGFR-mutant NSCLC after acquired resistance to prior osimertinib. Experimental arm is MET TKI + EGFR TKI; closest canonical class is 3rd-gen + chemo, but actually MET+EGFR-TKI; tagged as EGFR TKI 3rd-gen + chemotherapy as nearest match. May be considered partly out-of-scope (MET-amplified subset) but core eligibility is EGFR-mutant post-osimertinib.

ORCHARD: biomarker-directed phase 2 platform after first-line osimertinib progression. Multiple modules (Osi+savolitinib, Osi+gefitinib, Osi+necitumumab, carbo/pem/durva, Osi+alectinib, Osi+selpercatinib, etc.). Heterogeneous experimental arms; tagged 'investigational (other)' to reflect platform design.
Phase 1/2 of sunvozertinib (DZD9008) in EGFR or HER2 mutant NSCLC. Primary target population is EGFR Ex20ins, with HER2-mutant cohorts (HER2 out-of-scope). Mixed cohort; flag partially out-of-scope.

Out of scope (partial): SHP2 inhibitor RMC-4630 + cobimetinib arm enrolls KRAS/BRAF/NF1 (out-of-scope per protocol). RMC-4630 + osimertinib arm enrolls EGFR-mutant post-osimertinib NSCLC, which is in-scope. Mixed-scope; tagged investigational (other).

Phase 3 atezolizumab + carbo + paclitaxel + bev vs pemetrexed + platinum, in EGFR or ALK-driven NSCLC after prior TKI (post-osimertinib for T790M+; post-ALK TKI). IMpower150-style regimen; experimental arm class is IO + anti-VEGF + chemo; closest canonical is Anti-VEGF + chemotherapy.
Single-arm phase 2 of dacomitinib (2G EGFR TKI) with/without dose titration, first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
Phase 3 gefitinib + anlotinib (anti-angiogenic TKI) vs gefitinib + placebo, first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Closest canonical class is Bevacizumab + EGFR TKI (anti-angiogenic + EGFR TKI category).
FLAURA2: phase 3 osimertinib +/- platinum/pemetrexed first-line in EGFR-mutant NSCLC (Ex19del/L858R).
REZILIENT1: phase 1/2 CLN-081 (zipalertinib) in EGFR Ex20ins NSCLC post-platinum chemo (and prior approved Ex20ins TKI in Module C).
Phase 3 TAK-788 (mobocertinib) vs platinum chemo first-line in EGFR Ex20ins NSCLC.
Phase 3 ASK120067 (rezivertinib, 3G EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib, first-line EGFRm NSCLC.
Randomized phase 3 osimertinib +/- bevacizumab first-line in metastatic EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
Phase 2 osimertinib + aspirin vs osimertinib alone, first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Aspirin is investigational add-on; no clean canonical class — tagged investigational (other).

trial_name: SAF-189s / foritinib in ALK or ROS1 NSCLC
notes: Out of scope (partial): SAF-189s (foritinib) phase 1/2 in ALK-positive (in-scope) and ROS1-positive (OUT-of-scope) NSCLC; mixed cohorts. Includes prior ALK/ROS1 TKI-treated and naive patients.
Phase 3 SH-1028 (3G EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib first-line, EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
LASER301: phase 3 lazertinib (3G EGFR TKI) vs gefitinib first-line, EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

Out of scope: SKYSCRAPER-01 enrolls PD-L1-high, EGFR/ALK-negative driver-negative NSCLC. No oncogenic driver — out of scope for v3 EGFR/ALK NSCLC corpus. Drug class: tiragolumab + atezolizumab, closest canonical is PD-1/PD-L1 monotherapy (with anti-TIGIT).

OCELOT: phase 2 sequential osimertinib first-line -> chemo at progression -> osimertinib rechallenge third-line in EGFR-mutant NSCLC. Atypical EGFR mutations allowed; exon 20 insertions excluded. Drug class scored as 3G EGFR TKI as it's the first-line and sentinel intervention.

trial_name: Gefitinib + anlotinib in uncleared ctDNA EGFRm after gefitinib
notes: Phase 2: addition of anlotinib (anti-angiogenic) to ongoing first-line gefitinib in NSCLC with uncleared ctDNA EGFR-mut after gefitinib. Treatment intensification on gefitinib; closest canonical class is Bevacizumab + EGFR TKI.
Phase 2 osimertinib vs osimertinib + carbo/pemetrexed in EGFR-mutant NSCLC with detectable EGFR cfDNA after osimertinib initiation (early intensification).
Phase 2 gefitinib +/- bevacizumab specifically in EGFR L858R-mutant NSCLC (first-line).

Out of scope: CONTACT-01 atezolizumab + cabozantinib vs docetaxel in metastatic NSCLC after IO + platinum chemo; explicitly excludes EGFR sensitizing mutations and ALK fusions (driver-negative). Out of scope for v3 EGFR/ALK corpus.
MARIPOSA: phase 3 amivantamab + lazertinib vs osimertinib vs lazertinib monotherapy, first-line EGFR-mutant NSCLC (Ex19del/L858R).
v3 R1 fix: PAPILLON added; missed by F5 regex word-boundary bug for 'Exon 20ins'

Out of scope: ROME is a tumor-agnostic precision oncology basket. Explicitly excludes EGFR-mut and ALK-translocation NSCLC (and other 'well-established actionable targets with approved drugs'). Pan-tumor / actionable-target-not-actionable design — out of scope for v3 EGFR/ALK NSCLC corpus.
APPEAL: phase 2 almonertinib (aumolertinib, 3G EGFR TKI) +/- pemetrexed/carboplatin in T790M+ NSCLC after 1st/2nd-gen EGFR TKI.
v3 round-1 corrected: original supplementary NCT05009836 was savolitinib+osimertinib MET trial; real HERTHENA-Lung01 is NCT04619004.

v3 round-1 corrected: original supplementary NCT04644237 was DESTINY-Lung02 HER2-mutated; real TROPION-Lung01 is NCT04656652. Trial enrolled mixed biomarker NSCLC; partially in scope (EGFR-mut and AGA subgroups). | v3 R1 fix: tumor_type re-routed to NSCLC-EGFR (was NSCLC-other; trial enrolls broad pop, EGFR-mut subgroup is registrational)

trial_name: TOP (osimertinib +/- chemo in EGFR/TP53 co-mut NSCLC)
notes: TOP: phase 3 osimertinib +/- carbo/pemetrexed in untreated advanced non-squamous NSCLC with concurrent EGFR and TP53 mutations.

Multi-cohort: Cohort A driver-negative post-PD-1/L1 (out of scope), Cohort B EGFR/ALK post-TKI (in-scope). IBI318 (bispecific PD-1/PD-L1) + lenvatinib. Mixed scope; cohort B in-scope post-osimertinib/post-ALK-TKI.
SAINT: phase 3 anlotinib + icotinib vs icotinib in untreated EGFR concomitant-mutation NSCLC. Anti-angiogenic TKI + EGFR TKI; closest canonical class is Bevacizumab + EGFR TKI.
CAPLAND: randomised phase 2 osimertinib->dacomitinib vs dacomitinib->osimertinib sequencing in EGFR-mut NSCLC. First-line arm is 3G EGFR TKI.
ATTACK: phase 3 high-dose almonertinib vs osimertinib second-line in EGFR-mut NSCLC with brain metastases (post 1st/2nd-gen EGFR TKI).

Phase 3 almonertinib maintenance after definitive chemoradiation in Stage III unresectable EGFR-mut NSCLC. LAURA-like design; non-metastatic locally-advanced setting (partially out-of-scope for metastatic-only DAG, but EGFR-mut/post-CRT is a clearly defined NSCLC node).

trial_name: Almonertinib vs platinum chemo (uncommon EGFR first-line)
notes: Phase 3 almonertinib vs platinum chemo first-line in NSCLC with uncommon EGFR mutations (L861Q, G719X, or S768I).
MARIPOSA-2: phase 3 amivantamab + lazertinib + carbo/pemetrexed (or amivantamab + carbo/pem) vs carbo/pem in EGFR-mut NSCLC after osimertinib failure.
CLASSIC5: phase 2 chemo +/- ICI +/- bev in ALK-rearranged NSCLC after first-line alectinib failure. Tested role of ICI post-ALK TKI.

Phase 2 surufatinib + toripalimab + chemo in NSCLC: wild-type cohort (driver-negative first-line, partially out-of-scope) and EGFR T790M cohort post-osimertinib (in-scope). Mixed scope; tagged Anti-VEGF + chemotherapy (surufatinib is anti-angiogenic TKI).

SHR-1701 (PD-L1/TGF-beta bifunctional) + bevacizumab + pemetrexed/platinum vs comparators in EGFR-mutant non-squamous NSCLC after EGFR-TKI failure. Generation of prior EGFR TKI not specified; mapped to anti-VEGF + chemo class given backbone. Could alternatively be PD-L1 + bev + chemo combination.

JMT101 (anti-EGFR mAb) + osimertinib in EGFR exon 20 insertion NSCLC after platinum-based chemo. Concurrent sensitizing EGFR mutations excluded. Experimental backbone is osimertinib at 160 mg; classified as EGFR TKI 3rd-gen + add-on antibody.

Ivonescimab (AK112, PD-1/VEGF bispecific) + pemetrexed/carboplatin vs placebo + chemo in EGFR-mutant non-squamous NSCLC after EGFR-TKI failure. Prior systemic antitumor therapy other than EGFR-TKI excluded; prior immunotherapy excluded. Closest canonical class is anti-VEGF + chemo (with PD-1 dual targeting).

Brigatinib + carboplatin/pemetrexed vs brigatinib monotherapy as 1L in ALK-positive NSCLC. Prior TKI (any) excluded; concurrent EGFR/BRAF/KRAS/MET driver mutations excluded; adjuvant/consolidation therapy within 12 months excluded.
XZP-3621 (investigational 2nd-gen ALK TKI) vs crizotinib in treatment-naive ALK-positive NSCLC in China. No prior systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic disease; ages 18-75.

trial_name: NCT05215548 (Primary tumor resection + afatinib for stage IV NSCLC)
notes: Primary tumor resection in stage IV EGFR-mutant NSCLC without progression after 1L EGFR-TKI; afatinib used in study. Patients on initial afatinib (6-12 weeks) allowed; other prior EGFR-TKI excluded. Local control / surgical study, but anchored on EGFR-TKI backbone (afatinib).

Silevertinib (BDTX-1535) monotherapy in NSCLC with C797S acquired resistance after osimertinib or non-classical EGFR mutations (G719X, L861R, S768I). T790M and MET resistance excluded. Mapped to 3rd-gen EGFR TKI class (next-gen mutant-selective).

SY-3505 (3rd-gen ALK TKI) in ALK+ NSCLC. Phase I dose-escalation allows treatment-naive; dose-expansion and phase II require prior ALK TKI failure (alectinib or >=2 ALK TKIs). Classified as 3rd-gen ALK TKI.

Savolitinib + osimertinib vs platinum-based doublet chemotherapy in EGFR-mutant, MET-overexpressed/amplified NSCLC after osimertinib (1L or 2L) progression. Prior 3rd-gen EGFR TKI other than osimertinib excluded; prior MET inhibitors excluded.
Dacomitinib + anlotinib (anti-angiogenic) as 1L in EGFR L858R NSCLC (Phase IIb), Phase I also allows ex19del. T790M and other EGFR types excluded. No prior systemic therapy.

Furmonertinib (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) +/- chemo +/- bevacizumab as 1L in EGFR-mut (19Del/L858R) advanced NSCLC with uncleared ctDNA. EGFR ex20ins and any prior EGFR-TKI/systemic therapy excluded. Experimental arm includes 3rd-gen TKI + chemo.

Sacituzumab tirumotecan (SKB264, TROP2-ADC) combination therapy across cohorts: Cohort 1/2 EGFR-WT (1-2L or 1L), Cohort 3 EGFR-mut post-TKI. Trial spans driver-negative and EGFR-mut subgroups. Mapped to TROP2-ADC. Mixed scope: EGFR cohort fits NSCLC-EGFR; WT cohorts borderline-relevant.
TY-9591 (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) vs osimertinib as 1L in EGFR sensitive-mutation (ex19del/L858R) NSCLC. No prior systemic antitumor therapy.

Lazertinib + subcutaneous amivantamab vs lazertinib + IV amivantamab (formulation comparison) in EGFR-mut NSCLC progressed on osimertinib (or other 3rd-gen TKI) and platinum-based chemo. Both arms receive EGFR TKI 3rd-gen + bispecific.
JIN-A02 (4th-gen EGFR TKI targeting C797S/T790M) in EGFR-mutant advanced NSCLC after standard EGFR-TKI therapy (incl. osimertinib for Part C). Mapped to 3rd-gen class as closest canonical anchor.

HS-10376 monotherapy in advanced NSCLC with EGFR/HER2 exon 20 insertion mutations, after standard therapy invalid/intolerable. Prior EGFR/HER2 ex20ins inhibitors excluded. HER2-mut cohort partially out-of-scope per protocol but EGFR ex20ins cohort in-scope; classified investigational (other) given dual target.

Furmonertinib 240 mg QD in locally advanced/metastatic NSCLC with EGFR exon 20 insertion mutations after prior platinum-based chemotherapy (+/- PD-1/L1). Prior EGFR-targeting agents excluded. Furmonertinib is a 3rd-gen EGFR TKI.

EMB-01 (EGFR/cMET bispecific) + osimertinib in EGFR-mut NSCLC; Group 1: osimertinib 1L progression (ex19del/L858R), Group 2/3: T790M-persistent or lost after 2L+ osimertinib/3rd-gen TKI, Group 4: ex20ins. Mapped to amivantamab+lazertinib class (closest canonical EGFR/MET bispecific + EGFR TKI).

Sunvozertinib (DZD9008, EGFR exon 20 inhibitor) vs platinum-based doublet (pemetrexed + carboplatin) as 1L in EGFR ex20ins NSCLC. Concurrent classical EGFR mutations (19del/L858R/T790M/G719X/S768I/L861Q) excluded. Closest canonical class is 3rd-gen EGFR TKI (mutant-selective).

Sunvozertinib (DZD9008) monotherapy in pre-treated NSCLC with EGFR exon 20 insertion (1-3 prior lines including platinum). Prior ex20ins-targeting agents excluded. Mapped to 3rd-gen EGFR TKI (mutant-selective ex20ins inhibitor).

YK-029A (novel 3rd-gen EGFR TKI) in advanced NSCLC with T790M, ex20ins, or rare EGFR mutations (G719X/L861Q/S768I). Multi-cohort phase 1/2. Prior 3rd-gen EGFR TKI excluded for T790M/rare cohorts; prior ex20ins inhibitors excluded.

YK-029A vs platinum-based chemotherapy (pemetrexed + carbo/cis) as 1L in NSCLC with EGFR exon 20 insertion. Concurrent classical/T790M/rare EGFR mutations excluded; no prior systemic therapy for advanced disease.

SKB264 (Sac-TMT, TROP2-ADC) +/- pembrolizumab/carboplatin/osimertinib in advanced NSCLC. Cohorts include EGFR-WT/ALK-neg (1L) and EGFR-mut after TKI failure. Mapped to TROP2-ADC class. Mixed scope: EGFR cohort in-scope.

RC108 (MET-targeted ADC) + furmonertinib (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) +/- toripalimab (PD-1) in EGFR-mut NSCLC with MET expression (IHC 1+/2+/3+) after 1st/2nd/3rd-gen EGFR-TKI failure. Backbone is furmonertinib so mapped to 3rd-gen EGFR TKI class.
SY-3505 (3rd-gen ALK TKI) in ALK-positive NSCLC progressed on or intolerant to at least one 2nd-gen ALK TKI.

SKB264 (sacituzumab tirumotecan, TROP2-ADC) monotherapy vs pemetrexed + platinum in EGFR-sensitive-mutation non-squamous NSCLC after EGFR-TKI failure. Generation of prior TKI not specified. Prior TROP2-targeted or topoisomerase-I-targeting therapy excluded.
TGRX-326 (3rd-gen ALK TKI) monotherapy in ALK-positive NSCLC progressed on or intolerant to 2nd-gen ALK inhibitors. Prior 3rd-gen ALK inhibitors excluded.

Zipalertinib (TAS6417) + chemotherapy vs chemotherapy alone as 1L in EGFR exon 20 insertion or uncommon EGFR mutation (Part A) / ex20ins only (Part B) non-squamous NSCLC. Brief approved EGFR TKI exposure (<=8 weeks with documented progression) permitted; prior ex20ins-targeted therapy not allowed.

IN10018 (FAK inhibitor) + furmonertinib (3rd-gen EGFR TKI) in EGFR-mut NSCLC across 3 cohorts: Cohort 1 on furmonertinib 1L, Cohort 2 post 3rd-gen TKI + chemo, Cohort 3 treatment-naive. Backbone = 3rd-gen EGFR TKI.
TGRX-326 (3rd-gen ALK TKI) vs crizotinib in ALK-positive NSCLC, naive to ALK inhibitors (prior 1L chemotherapy with progression/intolerance allowed).

Lorlatinib monotherapy as 1L in advanced ALK-positive NSCLC in China (CROWN-criteria cohort plus other cohorts). No prior systemic therapy for advanced disease; ALK TKIs, angiogenesis inhibitors, IO and chemo all excluded as prior treatment.

Out of scope (partial): LMV-12 (HE003, MET/RET inhibitor) + osimertinib in NSCLC after 1L/2L/3L EGFR-TKI failure, Cohort A MET amplification, Cohort B RET fusion. RET-fusion cohort is out-of-scope per protocol; MET-amp cohort borderline. Backbone = osimertinib.
SY-3505 (3rd-gen ALK TKI) vs crizotinib in treatment-naive ALK-positive NSCLC. Patients with limited prior systemic therapy (up to 1 chemo regimen) may be allowed at investigator discretion.

trial_name: NCT06319950 (high-dose furmonertinib vs osimertinib, brain mets)
notes: High-dose furmonertinib 160 mg vs osimertinib 80 mg as 1L in EGFR-mut NSCLC with brain metastases. Prior EGFR TKI excluded; prior chemo allowed if no TKI received.
Sunvozertinib + anlotinib as 1L in EGFR-sensitive-mutation (19del/L858R/T790M) NSCLC with co-mutations (TP53/PIK3CA/CTNB1/RB1). Sunvozertinib classified as 3rd-gen EGFR TKI (mutant-selective).

JMT101 (anti-EGFR mAb) + osimertinib in stage IIIb-IV NSCLC with EGFR common mutations across 4 cohorts: (1) treatment-naive, (2) post 1st/2nd-gen TKI, (3) post 3rd-gen TKI no chemo, (4) post 3rd-gen + platinum. Backbone = osimertinib.

AK112 (ivonescimab, PD-1/VEGF bispecific) + pemetrexed/carboplatin vs placebo + chemo in EGFR-mutant non-squamous NSCLC after EGFR-TKI failure (1st/2nd-gen with T790M-neg in China; 3rd-gen for US/EU). Prior systemic chemo/IO/anti-angiogenic excluded. Class: anti-VEGF + chemo (PD-1/VEGF dual).

Dato-DXd +/- osimertinib vs platinum-based doublet chemotherapy in EGFR-mut NSCLC after osimertinib progression. Up to 2 prior EGFR TKIs (osimertinib only allowed 3rd-gen). No prior chemo/anti-VEGF/IO in metastatic setting.

FHND-9041 (novel EGFR-TKI) first-in-human Phase I/II in EGFR-mut NSCLC. Two groups: 2L/3L T790M-positive after 1st/2nd-gen EGFR TKI progression; and 1L treatment-naive EGFR-sensitive-mutation (ex19del/L858R).

Out of scope: EGFR-negative (driver-negative) NSCLC trial assessing olanzapine impact on 1L nivolumab efficacy. Per protocol decision rule 6, driver-negative NSCLC is out-of-scope for v3 (deferred to v4).
SAF-189s (foritinib succinate, 2nd-gen ALK TKI) vs crizotinib in 1L ALK-positive advanced/metastatic NSCLC. No prior systemic therapy for advanced disease; prior chemo/TKI within 14-28 days excluded.

Furmonertinib 160 mg QD as 1L in EGFR-classical-mut (19del or L858R) locally advanced/metastatic lung adenocarcinoma. Other driver oncogenes (ALK/ROS1/RET/BRAF/NTRK/MET/KRAS) excluded; no prior systemic therapy.

BEBT-109 in EGFR ex20ins NSCLC. Cohort 1: prior platinum chemo, no 3rd-gen TKI; Cohort 2: prior platinum + 3rd-gen TKI (osimertinib/savolitinib/alectinib >=80-110 mg). Prior ex20ins-targeted agents (poziotinib/TAK788/JNJ-372/CLN-081 or high-dose 3rd-gen TKI) excluded.

JMT101 (anti-EGFR mAb) + osimertinib vs osimertinib alone as 1L in EGFR-sensitive-mut non-squamous NSCLC. Concurrent ALK/ROS1/KRAS/BRAF/RET/MET/NTRK/HER2 actionable mutations excluded; no prior systemic therapy for advanced disease.

FHND9041 (novel EGFR-TKI targeting EGFR mutations) vs afatinib as 1L in advanced/metastatic EGFR-sensitive-mut (ex19del/L858R) NSCLC. Comparator is 2nd-gen TKI but experimental is described as 'novel' (likely 3rd-gen mutant-selective); mapped to 3rd-gen class.
